# Pounce

> A free browser side-scrolling cat platformer. Pick from four cat palettes
> and bound through a 240-tile hand-built level on the way to a cozy bed.
> Cat-food boxes power you up, magic fish unlock fishbone projectiles, you
> can stomp dogs and crawling kids — wasps you have to shoot or down-pounce.
> Vanilla HTML / CSS / JavaScript with no build step or install required;
> Web Audio synthesised soundtrack; global high-score leaderboard.

Pounce is an open-source browser game in the same family as
[cat-ski](https://ski.mike-lee.me/) — same author, same vanilla stack, same
plain-prose voice. Four selectable cat palettes (black, tabby, calico,
orange) with vector cat sprites shared with cat-ski. Hand-built tile level
structured around the four-act kishōtenketsu framework (intro → development
→ twist → conclusion) and the six 2D-platformer level patterns (Guidance,
Foreshadowing, Safe Zone, Layering, Branching, Pace Breaking).

The cat starts small and weak. Hitting a red `?`-style box pops a cat-food
can; eating it makes the cat big (one extra hit absorbed). Hitting a box
while big pops a magic fish; eating it grants the shooter state and the
ability to fire fishbone projectiles with the X key. Three obstacle animals
oppose: the dog (walking), the crawling small child (short, slow), and the
flying wasp — wasps cannot be stomped, only shot or down-pounced. Down-pounce
is a Mario-3-style ground-pound: in-air S or ↓ snaps the cat into a fast
straight-down slam that one-shots anything below, including wasps, for a
+200 score bonus.

Backed by Supabase for a global top-3 leaderboard, with a localStorage cache
so the strip paints instantly. Web Audio synthesised soundtrack (chiptune
I–V–vi–IV in C, 132 BPM) with a panic-mode tempo speedup at the last 30
seconds; M key toggles music. Installable as a Progressive Web App with an
offline service worker. Hosted on Vercel, source on GitHub.

## Pages

- [Play Pounce](https://pounce.mike-lee.me/): The game itself. Loads in any
  modern browser. Desktop primary; mobile is on the roadmap.
- [Building Pounce, a Browser Cat Platformer](https://pounce.mike-lee.me/readme.html):
  A walkthrough article covering the tile-based level architecture, the
  four-act kishōtenketsu pacing applied to the level, the vector cat sprites
  shared with cat-ski, and the free toolchain used to ship it.

## About the project

- Author: Mike Lee (Whisker Leaks)
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/mikelee89/
- Source code: https://github.com/ashtonmorrow/bros
- Sister projects: [cat-ski](https://ski.mike-lee.me/),
  [pear](https://pear.mike-lee.me/),
  [go](https://go.mike-lee.me/) — all by the same author at
  [mike-lee.me](https://mike-lee.me/)
- License: open source (see repository)
- Stack: vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript; HTML5 Canvas; Web Audio API;
  Supabase for the global leaderboard; no build step; no framework; no
  runtime dependencies beyond Google Fonts
- Hosting: Vercel, deployed automatically from the GitHub repository on push
- PWA: installable on iOS, Android, and desktop

## Optional

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/ashtonmorrow/bros): Issues, forks,
  and pull requests welcome.
- [Mike Lee on LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mikelee89/): Project
  author contact.
